title: Social Communication Questionnaire (SCQ-Lifetime)
description: A 40-item caregiver-report screening tool assessing communication skills and social functioning in children aged 4+ years. Based on published domain structure for social communication assessment.

scoring.scale: 0 or 1 per item (Yes/No — scoring direction varies)

severity_levels.label: clinical
severity_levels.description: Above clinical threshold — comprehensive ASD evaluation recommended

scoring.clinical_note: The SCQ is a screening tool intended to identify individuals who may benefit from a comprehensive diagnostic evaluation for ASD.

questions.text: Does your child now have phrases or sentences that he/she uses repeatedly in almost the same way (like quoting from a TV show)?
Can you have a back and forth 'conversation' with your child that involves taking turns or building on what you have said?
Has your child ever used odd phrases or said the same thing over and over in almost exactly the same way (either phrases he/she has heard others use or ones he/she has made up)?
Has your child ever used socially inappropriate questions or statements (e.g., asking someone personal questions or making personal comments at inappropriate times)?
Has your child ever got his/her pronouns mixed up (e.g., saying 'you' or 'he/she' for 'I')?
Has your child ever used words that he/she seemed to have invented or made up him/herself, or used odd, indirect or metaphorical ways of saying things?
Has your child ever said the same thing over and over in exactly the same way, or insisted that you say the same thing?
Has your child ever had things that he/she seemed to have to do in a very particular order or in a specific routine, and got upset if this was changed?
Has your child's facial expression usually seemed appropriate to the particular situation, as far as you could tell?
Has your child ever used your hand like a tool or as if it were part of his/her own body (e.g., pointing with your finger, putting your hand on a doorknob to get you to open the door)?
Has your child ever had any interests that preoccupy him/her and might seem odd to other people (e.g., traffic lights, drainpipes, timetables)?
Has your child ever seemed to be more interested in parts of a toy or an object (e.g., spinning the wheels of a car), rather than using the object as intended?
Has your child ever had any special interests that seemed unusual in their intensity but otherwise appropriate for his/her age and peer group?
Has your child ever seemed to be unusually interested in the sight, feel, sound, taste, or smell of things or people?
Has your child ever had any mannerisms or odd ways of moving his/her hands or fingers, such as flapping or moving his/her fingers in front of his/her eyes?
Has your child ever had any complicated movements of his/her whole body, such as spinning or repeatedly bouncing up and down?
Has your child ever deliberately hurt him/herself, such as biting his/her arm or banging his/her head?
Has your child ever had any objects (other than a soft toy or comfort blanket) that he/she had to carry around?
Does your child have any particular friends or a best friend?
When your child was 4-5 years old, did he/she ever talk with you just to be friendly (rather than to get something)?
When your child was 4-5 years old, did he/she ever spontaneously copy you or other people or what you/they were doing?
When your child was 4-5 years old, did he/she ever spontaneously point at things around him/her just to show you things (not because he/she wanted them)?
When your child was 4-5 years old, did he/she ever use gestures, other than pointing or pulling your hand, to let you know what he/she wanted?
When your child was 4-5 years old, did he/she nod his/her head to indicate 'yes'?
When your child was 4-5 years old, did he/she shake his/her head to indicate 'no'?
When your child was 4-5 years old, did he/she usually look at you directly in the face when doing things with you or talking with you?
When your child was 4-5 years old, did he/she smile back if someone smiled at him/her?
When your child was 4-5 years old, did he/she ever show you things that interested him/her to engage your attention?
When your child was 4-5 years old, did he/she ever offer to share things with you other than food?
When your child was 4-5 years old, did he/she ever seem to want you to join in with his/her enjoyment of something?
When your child was 4-5 years old, did he/she ever try to comfort you if you were sad or hurt?
When your child was 4-5 years old, when he/she wanted something or needed help, did he/she look at you and use gestures with sounds or words to get your attention?
When your child was 4-5 years old, did he/she show a normal range of facial expressions?
When your child was 4-5 years old, did he/she ever spontaneously join in and try to copy what you were doing in actions (such as vacuuming, gardening, or fixing things)?
When your child was 4-5 years old, did he/she ever play pretend or make-believe games?
When your child was 4-5 years old, did he/she seem interested in other children of approximately the same age?
When your child was 4-5 years old, did he/she ever respond positively when another child approached him/her?
When your child was 4-5 years old, if you came into a room and started talking to him/her without calling his/her name, did he/she usually look up and pay attention to you?
When your child was 4-5 years old, did he/she ever play imaginative games with another child in such a way that you could tell that each child understood what the other was pretending?
When your child was 4-5 years old, did he/she play cooperatively in group games of any kind?
